posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The US is monitoring increased tanker traffic through an alternative Omani shipping route around the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting efforts to maintain oil supply flows despite potential disruptions to the main Persian Gulf passage. The movement of multiple crude and LPG carriers on this route indicates efforts to sustain energy exports and market stability amid regional tensions.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister warned that deviations from current arrangements could complicate the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and heighten tensions, signaling potential risks to one of the world's critical oil transit chokepoints. The statement suggests Iran may resist alternative diplomatic solutions that differ from its current approach, with implications for global crude supply stability.

posts.text:

posts.text:
Iran Asserts Sole Control of Hormuz, Warns Challenges Will Bring More Violence
The comments by Iran’s foreign minister followed several days of back and forth fighting with the U.S. https://t.co/EIBe2cgNbN
posts.summary: Iran has asserted control over the Strait of Hormuz and warned that challenges to its authority will result in increased violence, following recent military exchanges with the U.S. This posturing raises concerns about potential disruptions to global oil shipments that transit through the strategically critical waterway.

posts.text:
